reasoning: Tanzania is broadly stable, and political violence fatalities are typically very low. For the baseline prior 360 days, the country saw around ~2 fatalities per 30-day window (~26 total for the year). The target threshold requires >31.67 fatalities (i.e., at least 32) in a single 30-day window, which would mean more deaths in a month than in the entire preceding year. There are no ongoing military campaigns or major crises in May 2025 that suggest hitting this threshold is imminent, keeping probabilities for the first three dates closely anchored to the minimum baseline of epistemic uncertainty, increasing slightly with time. However, general elections are scheduled for October 2025, overlapping precisely with the 30-day window for the Nov 7 resolution date. Although past elections (like 2020) were marred by violence, the death counts were generally below 20. Still, the heavily contested nature of the election meaningfully elevates the risk of a larger spike, justifying a higher probability for the final date.
